title: GIS education at Wuhan University
summary: Gengchen Mai's undergraduate education in Geographic Information System at Wuhan University provided the foundational knowledge in geospatial science that would later evolve into GeoAI research.
evidenceMove: The Wuhan chapter relies on biographical profile data. Direct institutional records from Wuhan University would strengthen this foundation chapter.
nextUpgrade: Wuhan University institutional record or graduation documentation would provide direct verification of undergraduate education.

title: UCSB doctoral research in GeoAI foundations
summary: M.A. and Ph.D. studies at UC Santa Barbara established Mai's expertise in Geographic Question Answering and Spatially-Explicit Machine Learning, with research at STKO Lab and industry internships.
evidenceMove: Strong chapter with direct UCSB profile and institutional connections. The graduate student page documents research focus and industry collaborations.
nextUpgrade: UCSB dissertation record or departmental thesis page would add formal academic documentation.

title: Stanford AI Lab bridges geography and AI
summary: Postdoctoral position at Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory represented a critical transition from pure geography to the intersection of AI and spatial data science.
evidenceMove: This chapter relies on biographical references. Direct Stanford institutional record would strengthen the postdoctoral chapter.
nextUpgrade: Stanford AI Lab profile page or departmental record would provide direct institutional verification.

title: Building a GeoAI research program at UGA
summary: First faculty position at University of Georgia established Mai as a leading researcher in GeoAI, with AAG Nystrom Award recognition and interdisciplinary affiliations.
evidenceMove: Strong chapter with institutional profiles and award documentation. The UGA period demonstrates research productivity and professional recognition.
nextUpgrade: UGA departmental news or research highlights would add institutional context to the faculty chapter.

title: SEAI Lab and the future of GeoAI
summary: Current position at UT Austin directs the Spatially Explicit Artificial Intelligence Lab, with NSF funding for geographic bias research and Google Research collaboration.
evidenceMove: Strongest chapter with multiple institutional sources, grant announcements, and lab documentation. This represents the present-tense research program.
nextUpgrade: Continue documenting research outputs, publications, and lab activities as the SEAI Lab develops.
